header.stance: U.S. fixed-income markets were closed for Juneteenth; global risks frame the next open
header.title: Daily FI Market Intelligence

header.stance: 美國固定收益市場因六月節休市；全球風險將主導下一次開市

zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 假日固定收益簡報

美國固定收益市場於 6 月 19 日因六月節休市。休市意味著沒有當日美國固定收益價格發現，也沒有可供重建的假日交易時段。資訊期間的主要發展，是將美伊臨時協議轉化為持久安排的進程受挫：原定週五舉行的永久和平談判延後。這項延遲使荷姆茲海峽能否不中斷通行，以及能源與運費成本能否持續走高，仍具不確定性；此前聯準會主席 Kevin Warsh 已在其首次政策會議上強調恢復物價穩定。

對下一次美國開市的意義在於前瞻性。持久的安排與可靠的荷姆茲海峽航運，可降低投入成本、限制商品與服務的第2輪通膨，並減輕對進口依賴型經濟體及美元融資借款人的壓力。延遲拉長或安全情勢再度中斷，則可能維持相反機制，使 Fed 較難忽略能源衝擊，並提高緊縮政策維持更久的風險。由於美國固定收益交易時段並未進行，這些發展均不能歸因於 6 月 19 日美國國債的走勢。

主要制衡力量在於，儘管最終談判延後，臨時協議仍是通往正常化的路徑。各方已建立重啟海峽的框架，未解問題在於執行，而非缺乏任何外交管道。通膨傳導也非必然：能源成本可能推升表面指標，卻未必演變成廣泛且持續的潛在通膨。美國市場重啟後，核心議題將是外交執行是否足夠強化以抑制此類傳導，抑或政策制定者將面對衝擊正在擴散的再度證據。

## 全球利率、信用與主權發展

由於能源不確定性出現在下一項關鍵通膨數據發布前，Fed 相關脈絡轉趨鷹派。5 月個人所得與支出數據將於 6 月 25 日公布，市場預估為 PCE 表面通膨月增 0.5%、核心通膨月增 0.3%。偏熱的結果將驗證 6 月會議所顯示的較緊縮反應函數；核心通膨廣度較弱，則支持能源衝擊規模龐大但尚未普遍化的觀點。市場仍流傳最終降息的預測，因此政策展望確實呈雙向，而非將 Warsh的言論轉化為預先確定的升息循環。

英國在美國休市期間提供最明確的即時主權市場佐證。Andy Burnham 在 Makerfield 勝選，開啟挑戰首相 Keir Starmer的途徑，並使投資人聚焦於財政框架的持久性。10 年期英國國債殖利率上升5個基點至 4.81%，表現落後歐洲同業。疑慮具條件性：Burnham 已承諾維持現有規則，但數名顧問支持拉長衡量期間，並為投資增加借款。現行債務規則已修訂過1次，在5年內創造最高達 £700億的額外借款能力，而 OECD 預估 2027 年公共債務將為 GDP的 105.4%。

英國消費使這場財政辯論對利率更具影響力。5 月零售銷售量月增 1.2%，年增 3.2%，此前 4 月數據經修正為下滑 1.0%。3個月指標上升 0.4%，非店面銷售量大增 6.1%。這是有意義的反彈，而非明確加速：前期萎縮及成長集中於網路消費，限制了訊號強度。即便如此，具韌性的家庭需求仍使英格蘭銀行較少理由忽略能源驅動的物價衝擊，尤其是在其最新會議中已有2位政策制定者主張立即升息之後。

歐元區證據指向相同的緊縮方向。德國生產者物價於 5 月上升 0.3%，年增 2.2%，高於 4 月的 1.7% 年增幅。剔除能源後，價格月增 0.7%、年增 2.3%；中間財年增 4.2%。ECB 首席經濟學家 Philip Lane 為近期將存款利率升至 2.25% 辯護，表示累積成本可能使通膨在 2026 年餘下期間維持在 3% 以上。他估計中性利率上緣約為 2.5%，意味著仍有再度行動空間，而不必然使政策明顯具緊縮性。

日本增添另一項通膨與買方基礎的挑戰。BOJ 副總裁冰見野良三警告，較快的企業間成本傳導可能擴散至消費者物價，並將潛在通膨推升至 2% 目標之上。5 月企業投入價格年增 6.3%，為3年來最快增速。能源補貼抑制最新消費者指標，但服務與加工食品成本仍然堅挺。冰見野也更直接地將貨幣疲弱與通膨相連，並主張延後必要調整可能迫使日後更急遽緊縮，從而加強 BOJ 在最近一次升息後繼續升息的承諾。

此一承諾受到政策委員會組成變化的限制。淺田統一郎對 6 月升息持異議，而隨著現任鷹派委員任期屆滿，額外的政府任命可能使委員會偏向寬鬆。這項緊張關係在經濟上相當重要：政治阻力可能拖慢正常化，但較弱貨幣將加劇進口通膨與家庭成本。目前經濟學家調查多將下一次升息集中於 10 月或 12 月，但委員會內部的分歧，使即將發布的薪資、服務價格及貨幣傳導，比基於日曆的預測更具資訊價值。

日本主權操作顯示承接運作正常，但不足以支持廣泛需求的結論。6 月 19 日3個月期國庫券標售，在提交的 JPY96,375億競標中，接受 JPY31503.8億的競爭性投標；加權平均殖利率為 0.9107%，最低接受價格的殖利率為 0.9224%。2項殖利率均低於 6 月 12 日標售，但未保留標售前基準。1項針對剩餘年期5年至11年的流動性增強操作，則以較參考利率 +0.016%的平均利差接受 JPY6,489億。這些都是標售結果，與 6 月 22 日發行日及即將到來的 JGB 供給不同。

加拿大 4 月零售數據在通膨發布前提供成長面的制衡。名目銷售月增 0.5% 至 C$730億，略低於 0.6%的市場預估，也慢於 3 月的 0.9% 增幅。銷售量持平，剔除燃料與汽車的核心指標下滑 0.7%；加油站業者與汽車經銷商帶動大部分表面增幅。5 月初步指標指向上升 1.0%，但僅基於受訪公司中 52.1%的回覆。因此，加拿大央行的利率路徑仍取決於較高能源成本是否推升物價，卻未帶來相應的實質需求。

信用供給仍可取得，但日益集中在策略性投資與政策支持。SpaceX的銀行團正籌備潛在、至少 $200億的投資級債券發行，以資助太空中的人工智慧營運與資料中心。該交易尚屬前瞻性，在此期間未確定定價、訂單簿或最終到期條款，因此其訊號是未來對存續期間承接能力的大型檢驗，而非已完成的吸收。在印度，至少4家放款機構計畫透過年定價 1.5%的印度央行換匯窗口，發行約 $25億的美元債，這是在貸款成長超越存款吸收之處，針對性緩解融資壓力的方式。

俄羅斯逆著更廣泛的緊縮格局調降政策利率，但降幅低於預期。較強的盧布及 5 月 5.3%的通膨、為近3年來最低水準，創造寬鬆空間。其持久性受制於擴大的財政赤字、戰時支出及 2026 年成長預測下修至 0.4%。此決策說明更廣泛的主權模式：能源出口國可從同一地緣政治衝擊取得短期通膨放緩與收入支持，而該衝擊卻提高進口國的政策壓力；但財政擴張仍可使中期利率風險居高不下。

## 下一次開市觀察

美國價格發現將於 6 月 22 日恢復，同時公布發行 $890億的 13 週期國庫券及 $770億的 26 週期國庫券，兩者均於 6 月 25 日交割。6 月 23 日接續發行 $690億的2年期國庫券、6 月 24 日 $700億的5年期國庫券，以及 6 月 25 日 $440億的7年期國庫券。可觀察的檢驗在於，隨著 PCE 發布，現金利率是否維持有序，以及直接與間接需求是否避免交易商承接比例異常偏高。廣泛吸收加上核心通膨趨緩，將削弱即將升息的論點；通膨偏熱或2年至7年期需求不佳則會強化該論點，並將壓力轉向對政策敏感的存續期間。

能源傳導將首先在美國以外接受檢驗。中國 6 月 22 日的 6 月貸款市場報價利率定價，公開基準為1年期維持 3.00%、5年期維持 3.50% 不變。加拿大當日稍晚公布的 5 月 CPI，預期年增率將由 2.8% 升至 3.0%；澳洲 5 月 CPI 則於 6 月 24 日公布，先前讀數為月增 0.4%、年增 4.2%。核心指標、貨幣與當地前端若保持穩定，將支持恢復石油流動可抑制第2輪效應的觀點。更廣泛的服務或食品通膨則將推翻這項制約，並加強全球資本競爭。

日本與英國提供主權信用度檢驗。日本將於 6 月 23 日發行約 JPY25,000億的5年期債券，並於 6 月 25 日增額發行約 JPY7,000億的 20 年期債券。投標倍數、接受價格及需求分布將顯示，隨 BOJ 正常化，國內買方能否吸收存續期間。在英國，觸發因素是具體的領導與財政規則指引，而非圍繞人物的揣測。有序的 JGB 標售及已有資金支應的英國政策框架，將限制外國期限溢價外溢；日本需求疲弱或出現可信的額外未有資金支應借款訊號，則會在美國市場完成假日窗口重新定價前，挑戰該論點。
zh-TW.methodology_note: SIFMA 建議六月節期間美國固定收益市場全面休市。未將前一交易日市場水準呈列為當日走勢。

overall_summary.headline: 假日休市使全球通膨與主權風險留待下一次美國開市消化
overall_summary.session_narrative: 美國固定收益市場因六月節休市，因此該期間沒有當日國內價格發現。延後舉行的美伊永久和平談判使荷姆茲海峽、能源成本及通膨傳導風險未解，而英國、歐元區及日本較強的通膨訊號則對政策寬鬆構成挑戰。臨時協議仍提供回歸正常化的路徑，且傳導並非必然。在下一次開市時，美國國債供給、核心通膨及海外主權信用度將顯示緊縮政策壓力是在擴大還是緩和。
overall_summary.summary: 六月節期間，美國固定收益市場沒有價格發現，延後舉行的美伊和平談判、能源傳導，以及全球主權壓力將影響下一次開市。展望仍呈雙向：荷姆茲海峽航運恢復與通膨趨緩將減輕壓力；再度中斷則會延長緊縮政策。

section_summaries.market_read: 假日使下一次美國開市暴露於未解的能源與政策傳導風險。
section_summaries.next_check: 觀察和平談判是否恢復，以及可靠的荷姆茲海峽航運能否抑制更廣泛的通膨。
section_summaries.summary: 美國市場休市之際，延後舉行的美伊永久和平談判，使下一次開市前荷姆茲海峽航運可靠性與能源傳導仍未明朗。臨時框架仍提供回歸正常化的路徑，關鍵條件則在於較高的表面成本是否擴散為持續性的潛在通膨。

section_summaries.market_read: 海外通膨與財政風險提高期限溢價維持高檔的機率。
section_summaries.next_check: 追蹤核心通膨廣度、BOJ 成本傳導，以及已有資金支應的英國財政指引。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球利率、信用與主權發展
section_summaries.summary: 英國需求具韌性、歐元區上游價格走強，以及日本成本傳導，均對寬鬆構成挑戰；主權信用度則仍不均衡，全球利率偏向緊縮。此訊號受到美國通膨雙向風險、政治對 BOJ 正常化的限制，以及加拿大實質需求偏弱所制約。

section_summaries.market_read: 供給有序且通膨受控，將限制對政策敏感型存續期間的壓力。
section_summaries.next_check: 關注美國標售的交易商承接比例，並同步觀察核心 PCE 與海外存續期間需求。
section_summaries.summary: 下一次美國開市同時面對大量短票與附息債供給，以及即將發布的 PCE 檢驗，使標售分配與核心通膨成為最清楚的國內訊號。海外方面，中國、加拿大、澳洲、日本與英國的結果將顯示能源傳導與主權信用度是否加劇全球資本競爭。

watch_events.market_implication: 疲弱標售或偏熱 PCE 將加強緊縮政策對前端存續期間的壓力。
watch_events.market_trigger: 交易商承接比例異常偏高、存續期間需求疲弱，或核心數據偏熱。
watch_events.title: 美國國債供給與 PCE 檢驗
watch_events.watch_line: 監測2年至7年期標售需求，以及 5 月核心 PCE 通膨的廣度。

watch_events.market_implication: 持續傳導將加強全球緊縮壓力與資本競爭。
watch_events.market_trigger: 服務或食品通膨擴大、貨幣不穩定，或當地前端承壓。
watch_events.title: 全球能源傳導檢驗
watch_events.watch_line: 比較中國利率定價與加拿大及澳洲通膨廣度。

watch_events.market_implication: 任一結果都將提高外國期限溢價對美國重新定價窗口的外溢。
watch_events.market_trigger: 日本存續期間需求疲弱，或出現可信的額外未有資金支應英國借款訊號。
watch_events.title: 日本與英國主權信用度
watch_events.watch_line: 評估 JGB 標售投標倍數與接受價格，並觀察具體的英國財政規則指引。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Holiday Fixed-Income Brief

U.S. fixed-income markets were closed on June 19 for Juneteenth. The closure meant there was no same-day U.S. fixed-income price discovery and no holiday session to reconstruct. The information window's primary development was a setback in converting the interim U.S.-Iran agreement into a durable settlement: permanent-peace talks scheduled for Friday were postponed. That delay preserved uncertainty over uninterrupted passage through the Strait of Hormuz and the persistence of energy and freight costs just after Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh emphasized restoring price stability at his first policy meeting.

The significance for the next open U.S. session is prospective. A lasting settlement and dependable shipping through Hormuz could ease input costs, limit second-round inflation across goods and services, and reduce the pressure on import-dependent economies and dollar-funded borrowers. Prolonged delays or renewed security disruption could sustain the opposite mechanism, leaving the Fed less scope to look through the energy shock and raising the risk that restrictive policy lasts longer. None of those developments can be attached to a June 19 Treasury move because the U.S. fixed-income session did not occur.

The main counterforce is that the interim agreement remains a route toward normalization even though final negotiations have slipped. The parties had established a framework to reopen the strait, and the unresolved question is execution rather than the absence of any diplomatic channel. Inflation transmission is also not automatic: energy costs can lift headline measures without becoming broad, persistent underlying inflation. When U.S. markets reopen, the central issue will be whether diplomatic implementation strengthens enough to contain that pass-through, or whether policy makers instead face renewed evidence that the shock is spreading.

## Global Rates, Credit, And Sovereign Developments

The Fed thread hardened because energy uncertainty arrived ahead of the next decisive inflation release. May personal income and outlays are due June 25, with consensus estimates of 0.5% monthly headline PCE inflation and 0.3% core inflation. A hot result would validate the more restrictive reaction function signaled at the June meeting. Softer core breadth would instead support the view that the energy shock is large but not becoming generalized. Forecasts for eventual rate cuts remain in circulation, which keeps the policy outlook genuinely two-sided rather than converting Warsh's rhetoric into a predetermined hiking cycle.

Britain supplied the clearest live sovereign-market confirmation during the U.S. closure. Andy Burnham's Makerfield victory opened a path to challenge Prime Minister Keir Starmer and focused investors on the durability of the fiscal framework. 10-year gilt yields rose 5 basis points to 4.81%, underperforming European peers. The concern is conditional: Burnham has committed to the existing rules, but several advisers favor longer measurement horizons and more borrowing for investment. The current debt rule has already been revised once to create as much as £70 billion of additional borrowing capacity over 5 years, and OECD projections put public debt at 105.4% of GDP in 2027.

UK consumption made that fiscal debate more consequential for rates. Retail-sales volumes rose 1.2% in May and 3.2% from a year earlier after April was revised to a 1.0% decline. The 3-month measure gained 0.4%, and non-store volumes jumped 6.1%. This was a meaningful rebound rather than a clean acceleration: the prior contraction and the concentration in online spending limit the signal. Even so, resilient household demand gives the Bank of England less reason to look through an energy-driven price impulse, especially after two policymakers had sought an immediate rate increase at its latest meeting.

Euro-area evidence pointed in the same restrictive direction. German producer prices increased 0.3% in May and 2.2% from a year earlier, up from a 1.7% annual pace in April. Excluding energy, prices rose 0.7% on the month and 2.3% annually; intermediate goods gained 4.2% over the year. ECB Chief Economist Philip Lane defended the recent increase in the deposit rate to 2.25%, saying accumulated costs could keep inflation above 3% through the remainder of 2026. His estimate that the upper end of neutral is about 2.5% leaves room for another move without necessarily making policy clearly contractionary.

Japan added a separate inflation-and-buyer-base challenge. BOJ Deputy Governor Ryozo Himino warned that faster business-to-business cost pass-through could broaden into consumer prices and push underlying inflation beyond the 2% target. Corporate input prices rose 6.3% from a year earlier in May, the fastest pace in 3 years. Energy subsidies restrained the latest consumer measure, but services and processed-food costs remained firm. Himino also linked currency weakness more directly to inflation and argued that delaying necessary adjustments could force sharper tightening later, reinforcing the BOJ's commitment to further increases after its latest rate rise.

That commitment is constrained by the policy board's changing composition. Toichiro Asada dissented from the June increase, and additional government appointments may tilt the board toward accommodation as current hawks' terms expire. The tension is economically important: political resistance may slow normalization, yet a weaker currency would intensify imported inflation and household costs. Current economist surveys cluster the next increase around October or December, but the disagreement inside the board makes incoming wages, services prices and currency pass-through more informative than a calendar-based forecast.

Japanese sovereign operations showed functioning absorption without supporting a broad demand verdict. The June 19 3-month bill auction accepted JPY3,150.38 billion of competitive bids from JPY9,637.50 billion submitted, at a 0.9107% weighted-average yield and 0.9224% at the lowest accepted price. Both yields were below the June 12 sale, but no pre-auction benchmark was retained. A separate liquidity-enhancement operation for 5- to 11-year remaining maturities accepted JPY648.9 billion at a +0.016% average spread to the reference rate. These are auction results, distinct from the June 22 issue date and from forthcoming JGB supply.

Canada's April retail data provided a growth counterweight ahead of inflation. Nominal sales increased 0.5% to C$73.0 billion, slightly below the 0.6% consensus estimate and slower than March's 0.9% gain. Sales volumes were unchanged and the core measure excluding fuel and autos fell 0.7%; gasoline vendors and vehicle dealers generated much of the headline increase. A preliminary May indicator pointed to a 1.0% rise, though it was based on responses from only 52.1% of surveyed companies. The Bank of Canada's rates path therefore remains sensitive to whether higher energy costs lift prices without equivalent real demand.

Credit supply stayed available but increasingly concentrated around strategic investment and policy support. SpaceX bankers were preparing a potential investment-grade bond sale of at least $20 billion to fund artificial-intelligence operations and data centers in space. The transaction was prospective, with no pricing, orderbook or final maturity terms established in the window, so it signals a large future test of duration capacity rather than completed absorption. In India, at least 4 lenders planned roughly $2.5 billion of dollar issuance using an RBI swap window priced at 1.5% annually, a targeted way to relieve funding pressure where loan growth has outpaced deposit gathering.

Russia moved against the broader tightening map by lowering its policy rate, though by less than expected. A stronger ruble and May inflation of 5.3%, the slowest in almost 3 years, created room to ease. The durability is limited by a widening fiscal deficit, wartime expenditure and a 2026 growth forecast cut to 0.4%. The decision illustrates the wider sovereign pattern: an energy exporter can obtain near-term disinflation and revenue support from the same geopolitical shock that raises policy pressure for importers, but fiscal expansion can keep medium-term rates risk elevated.

## Next Open-Session Watch

U.S. price discovery resumes June 22 alongside announced sales of $89 billion of 13-week bills and $77 billion of 26-week bills, both settling June 25. A $69 billion 2-year note follows June 23, a $70 billion 5-year note June 24 and a $44 billion 7-year note June 25. The observable test is whether cash rates remain orderly and direct and indirect demand prevent unusually heavy dealer awards as the PCE release arrives. Broad absorption plus softer core inflation would weaken the case for an imminent hike; hot inflation or poor 2- to 7-year demand would strengthen it and shift the pressure toward policy-sensitive duration.

Energy pass-through will be tested first outside the United States. China's June loan-prime-rate fixing on June 22 has a the consensus expectation of no change from 3.00% for 1 year and 3.50% for 5 years. Canada's May CPI later that day is expected to rise to 3.0% annually from 2.8%, and Australia's May CPI follows June 24 after prior readings of 0.4% monthly and 4.2% annually. Stable core measures, currencies and local front ends would support the view that restored oil flows can contain second-round effects. Broader services or food inflation would overturn that restraint and reinforce global competition for capital.

Japan and the UK provide the sovereign credibility test. Japan will sell about JPY2,500 billion of 5-year bonds on June 23 and reopen about JPY700 billion of 20-year bonds on June 25. Bid coverage, accepted pricing and the distribution of demand will show whether domestic buyers can absorb duration as the BOJ normalizes. In Britain, the trigger is concrete leadership and fiscal-rule guidance rather than speculation around personalities. Orderly JGB auctions and a funded UK policy framework would limit the foreign term-premium spillover; weak Japanese demand or credible signs of additional unfunded borrowing would challenge that thesis before U.S. markets finish repricing the holiday window.

section_summaries.market_read: The holiday leaves the next U.S. open exposed to unresolved energy and policy transmission.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch whether peace talks resume and dependable Hormuz shipping contains broader inflation.
section_summaries.summary: With the U.S. session shut, postponed U.S.-Iran permanent-peace talks left Hormuz reliability and energy pass-through unresolved ahead of the next open. The interim framework still offers a route to normalization, while the key qualifier is whether higher headline costs broaden into persistent underlying inflation.

section_summaries.market_read: Overseas inflation and fiscal risks increase the chance that term premia stay elevated.
section_summaries.next_check: Track core inflation breadth, BOJ pass-through, and funded UK fiscal guidance.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Rates, Credit, And Sovereign Developments
section_summaries.summary: Global rates leaned restrictive as resilient UK demand, firmer euro-area pipeline prices, and Japanese cost pass-through challenged easing, while sovereign credibility remained uneven. The signal is qualified by two-sided U.S. inflation risks, political constraints on BOJ normalization, and weaker Canadian real demand.

section_summaries.market_read: Orderly supply and contained inflation would limit pressure on policy-sensitive duration.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch dealer awards in U.S. auctions alongside core PCE and overseas duration demand.
section_summaries.summary: The next U.S. open combines heavy bill and coupon supply with a nearby PCE test, making auction distribution and core inflation the clearest domestic signals. Overseas, Chinese, Canadian, Australian, Japanese, and UK outcomes will show whether energy pass-through and sovereign credibility intensify global competition for capital.

methodology_note: SIFMA recommends a full U.S. fixed-income market close for Juneteenth. No prior-session market levels are presented as current-day moves.

overall_summary.headline: Holiday closure leaves global inflation and sovereign risks for the next U.S. open
overall_summary.session_narrative: U.S. fixed-income markets were closed for Juneteenth, so the window produced no same-day domestic price discovery. Postponed U.S.-Iran peace talks kept Hormuz, energy-cost, and inflation-pass-through risks unresolved, while firmer UK, euro-area, and Japanese inflation signals challenged policy easing. The interim agreement still offers a route to normalization, and pass-through is not automatic. At the next open, Treasury supply, core inflation, and overseas sovereign credibility will show whether restrictive policy pressure is broadening or easing.
overall_summary.summary: No U.S. fixed-income price discovery occurred on Juneteenth, leaving postponed U.S.-Iran peace talks, energy pass-through, and global sovereign pressure to shape the next open. The outlook remains two-sided: restored Hormuz flows and softer inflation would ease pressure, while renewed disruption would prolong restrictive policy.

watch_events.market_implication: A weak auction or hot PCE would reinforce restrictive policy pressure on front-end duration.
watch_events.market_trigger: Unusually heavy dealer awards, weak duration demand, or a hot core reading.
watch_events.title: U.S. Treasury Supply and PCE Test
watch_events.watch_line: Monitor 2- to 7-year auction demand and the breadth of May core PCE inflation.

watch_events.market_implication: Persistent pass-through would reinforce global tightening pressure and competition for capital.
watch_events.market_trigger: Broader services or food inflation, unstable currencies, or pressured local front ends.
watch_events.title: Global Energy Pass-Through Test
watch_events.watch_line: Compare China’s rate fixing with Canadian and Australian inflation breadth.

watch_events.market_implication: Either outcome would raise foreign term-premium spillover into the U.S. repricing window.
watch_events.market_trigger: Weak Japanese duration demand or credible signs of additional unfunded UK borrowing.
watch_events.title: Japan and UK Sovereign Credibility
watch_events.watch_line: Assess JGB auction coverage and accepted pricing alongside concrete UK fiscal-rule guidance.
